itinerary.title: Hanoi Discovery – Temples, Legends & Colonial Charm
itinerary.description: Your full-day exploration begins at the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, the resting place of the revered leader. Nearby, his House on Stilts offers a modest glimpse into the lifestyle of a man who guided a nation.At the One Pillar Pagoda, dating back to 1049, you’ll see one of Vietnam’s most iconic religious sites—symbolizing purity rising from a lotus pond. The peaceful Tran Quoc Pagoda, Hanoi’s oldest Buddhist temple, stands gracefully by West Lake.After lunch, stroll through the poetic grounds of the

itinerary.title: Hanoi – Sapa – Cat Cat Village - Muong Hoa valley
itinerary.description: At 7:30, we will whisk you away from Hanoi towards Sapa. As you journey along the new highway, be captivated by the picturesque views of rural countryside, mountains, and the Red River.Arrive in Sapa around 13:10-13:20 and transfer to your hotel. After lunch, venture to Cat Cat village, nestled at the foot of Fansipan Peak and home to the Black H’mong ethnic group. Explore their daily life activities and trek down to the valley bottom to admire the waterfall, a relic of the French hydroelectric

itinerary.title: Sapa Trekking - Hanoi
itinerary.description: Commence your day with a 9:30 am departure from the hotel, taking a short road transfer out of Sapa town to the walking path. Embark on a two-hour uphill walk along a broad path, ascending the Den Thang range. Enjoy breathtaking views of Fansipan Peak and the Muong Hoa Valley as the trek leads to Hang Da Village of the H’mong, perched at a 1,800m hilltop, overlooking the Muong Hoa Valley.Explore the village, break for lunch, and continue the trek downhill through H’mong hamlets of the Hau Thao C
activities: Commence your day with a 9:30 am departure from the hotel, taking a short road transfer out of Sapa town to the walking path

itinerary.title: Ninh Binh Wonders – Limestone Landscapes & Ancient Capitals
itinerary.description: Leave behind the lively rhythm of modern Hanoi and venture south into the serene, poetic landscapes of Ninh Binh—a region often hailed as “Halong Bay on land.” Your journey begins in Hoa Lu, the ancient capital of Vietnam, where timeworn temples rest beneath towering karst cliffs, and the air seems to carry whispers of bygone kings and noble warriors. Wander through the sacred grounds of the Dinh and Le Dynasty Temples, gracefully nestled in a lush valley embraced by nature’s dramatic grandeur.C
activities: Wander through the sacred grounds of the Dinh and Le Dynasty Temples, gracefully nestled in a lush valley embraced by nature’s dramatic grandeur

itinerary.title: Hanoi – Halong Bay
itinerary.description: Leave the bustle of Hanoi behind as you journey through the Red River Delta, passing by vibrant rice fields, riverside villages, and farmers guiding their water buffalo. Arrive at Tuan Chau Harbor, where your elegant cruise vessel awaits to carry you through the magical seascape of Halong Bay and the tranquil waters of Lan Ha Bay.As your boat glides past towering limestone pillars rising from emerald waters, enjoy a freshly prepared seafood lunch served on deck. In the afternoon, paddle a kayak
activities: As your boat glides past towering limestone pillars rising from emerald waters, enjoy a freshly prepared seafood lunch served on deck
In the afternoon, paddle a kayak

itinerary.title: Ha Long - Ha Noi - Ho Chi Minh
itinerary.description: Begin your morning with a gentle Tai Chi session on the cruise deck, as the first light of day bathes the bay’s majestic limestone peaks in gold. After a hearty breakfast, set out to explore one of the area’s highlights—perhaps the impressive Cave, known for its striking stalactites and dramatic stone formations, or visit a peaceful fishing village, where the rhythms of coastal life unfold amidst scenic surroundings. Each experience offers a unique window into the natural and cultural beauty of
activities: Begin your morning with a gentle Tai Chi session on the cruise deck, as the first light of day bathes the bay’s majestic limestone peaks in gold
Each experience offers a unique window into the natural and cultural beauty of

itinerary.title: Tunnels & Towers – Cu Chi and the Spirit of Ho Chi Minh
itinerary.description: Begin your day with a journey to the historic Cu Chi Tunnels, an extensive underground network used by Vietnamese soldiers during the war. Walk through reconstructed chambers, see hidden entrances, and learn about the endurance and ingenuity that defined resistance.Back in Ho Chi Minh City, explore architectural and cultural landmarks: the Reunification Palace, once the seat of South Vietnam’s government; the Notre Dame Cathedral, built entirely of French-imported materials; and the charming Cen
activities: Begin your day with a journey to the historic Cu Chi Tunnels, an extensive underground network used by Vietnamese soldiers during the war
Walk through reconstructed chambers, see hidden entrances, and learn about the endurance and ingenuity that defined resistance

itinerary.title: Life Along the Water – Mekong Delta Day Trip from Ho Chi Minh City
itinerary.description: Depart bustling Ho Chi Minh City this morning and journey south to Ben Tre, the verdant gateway to the Mekong Delta, often called the “rice bowl” of Vietnam. Upon arrival, board a small wooden boat to explore the intricate web of waterways that nourish this lush region. As you cruise past coconut palms, fruit orchards, and riverside villages, life on the river unfolds with a quiet grace.Disembark at riverside workshops where age-old techniques are still practiced. Watch as bricks are shaped by h

itinerary.title: Farewell Vietnam – Memories to Carry Home
itinerary.description: Savor your final moments in Vietnam. Enjoy a quiet morning with a stroll through Tao Dan Park, or explore a local coffee shop for one last ca phe sua da (Vietnamese iced coffee). Depending on your flight time, you may stop at a market for souvenirs or revisit a favorite café. At the appointed time, your driver will transfer you to Tan Son Nhat International Airport for your onward journey.Highlight: Farewell to a land of grace, memory, and wonder.Meals, Activities & StayHo Chi Minh Breakfast at
